title: Preparation of aqueous extracts of plants
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bakbv","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dj0jj","text":"Aqueous extracts of plants are a simple, economical and eco-friendly alternative to use as a source of antifungal activity. Here is a protocol for extracting any type of plant material (leaves) with water using a simple blender to grind the dried leaves with water and then a series of centrifuges to eliminate the solid waste, finally a series of filtrations to have an aqueous extract sterile. This aqueous extract can be stored at 4 ° C and depending on the plant the activity can be maintained for a fixed period of time. After this process the aqueous extract is ready to be used in bioassays of antifungal activity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7342v","text":"1.- Falcon tubes are reused (clean) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4i2te","text":"2.- Eppendorf tubes are reused (clean)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ffb9r","text":"3.- Filter holder (swinnex)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eqjfu","text":"4.- Durapore, Membrane filters from Millipore (25 mm)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6brjo","text":" 0.65 μm DV","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e3c9o","text":"0.45 μm HV","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f7f0l","text":"0.22 μm GV ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8napr","text":"5.-Stove with 50 ° C temperature capacity","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1c6d6","text":"Sterilize: water, filter holder (swinnex) prepared with the 0.22 μm GV","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: -Collection and preparation of leaves for drying Collect fresh leaves from the field In the lab wash them with tap water Remove excess water with a kitchen centrifuge
-Drying of leaves Distribute the leaves in cardboard boxes Put them at 50 °C in a stove for 2 -3 days Store them in plastic bags until use
1. Blend in a laboratory blender at the maximum speed undetermined, dry leaves 00:01:30, every 30 sec stir with a spatula
4. Take the supernatant and pour it into a container, take 1800 μL in 2 mL eppendorf tubes, use the necessary tubes 5. centrifuge 00:30:00, at 20817 g, room temperature
3. Pour the mixture into 50 mL falcon tubes and centrifuge 00:30:00, at 3220 g Undetermined
2. Add undetermined, sterile distilled water 00:01:30, mix everything

6. Take the supernatant of all the tubes and pour it into a container 7. Filter the aqueous extract with the membrane of 0.65 μm contained in a swinnex 8. Take the previous filtering and filter it with the membrane of 0.45 μm contained in a swinnex

9. In a laminar flow hood, sterilize the aqueous extract by filtration using a membrane of 0.22 μm contained in a swinnex 10. Store the aqueous extract in a sterile 50 mL falcon tube in the dark at 4 ° C until its use.
creator: Leticia LPE Peraza Echeverría
